title: Printing a large Gantt without losing quality and hierarchies
excerpt: Is there a way to print a large Gantt chart without losing quality and hierarchies? The PDF option produces a single screenshot, it automatically reduces the time window by autofitting, loses quality if you zoom in and d…

posts.author: nscaggiante (Nicola)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there a way to print a large Gantt chart without losing quality and hierarchies? The PDF option produces a single screenshot, it automatically reduces the time window by autofitting, loses quality if you zoom in and doesn’t show hierarchies. We really need to be able to print PDFs similar to Microsoft Project as we work with public administrations and they do not accept any format other than PDFs.

The last discussion on the topic was in 2024 and even by replying there Ididn’t receive any feedback. If we are left without an option we must go back to Project which would be really bad…

Thanks

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @nscaggiante ,

I wanted to mention another option for those who collaborate on timelines with clients, contractors, or other third parties outside of monday.com.

Export Gantt → attach PDF → send email → wait for feedback → update timeline → export again → resend.

Repeat. And repeat. And repeat.

Somewhere along the way:

• no one remembers which version is the latest
• timeline changes get buried in long emails
• approvals take days
• testing gets delayed
• last-minute pressure before launch becomes inevitable

We developed the Interactive Gantt Timeline app, that allows to share Gantt with clients and collaborate directly on it:

  • Clients can leave comments and suggest timeline changes right on Gantt

    leave suggestions2045×1045 312 KB

  • PMs get notification about comments and suggestions, and can test different scenarios, and then approve the appropriate timeline.

    test versions gantt1581×993 212 KB

After approving, new timeline automatically updates the board.

Interactive Gantt Timeline doesn’t replace monday.com’s native Gantt — it enhances it.
Keep using monday.com’s Gantt view internally, and share Interactive Gantt Timeline with clients outside your workspace.

No version chaos.
No endless PDF exports.
Just one live Gantt for monday.com.

You can install and test the app on your account.

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @nscaggiante,

As a workaround, we export the Gantt to Excel and then use an AI tool to format it into a clean one-page (or multi-page) PDF. We use Copilot, but any AI tool should work. It’s not ideal, but it gets the job done for now.
